subject: WM-0166 blocked
body: Two High PR-1 hard gates require an independent product/runtime repair task before WM-0166 can resume: (1) prove a real Worker-owned scheduler survives and advances continuously for 600 wall-clock seconds at normal speed, with cadence/backpressure evidence and no UI advance/drain/command-drain clock; current Chromium smoke reaches only tick >=9 while 18000 ticks are headless explicit stepping; (2) repair the runtime/publication lifecycle so non-instant work exposes moving and working states across multiple Worker publications under the 3-tick scheduler quantum; current lifecycle transitions within tick 0 and completes by tick 3, so publications observe idle only. Create a focused simulation-engineer/sim-worker product repair task owning required sim-core/sim-worker production paths, followed by qa-performance focused real Worker and default Web evidence. Do not lower thresholds or change protocol/schema/save.
